guide_agentcard_p_cardholder: Once you set up an Agent Card, your agent's card appears in the Card Holder. Other users can:
guide_agentcard_ch_li1: ✅ Search for your agent via Public Code
guide_agentcard_ch_li2: ✅ Save your card to their Card Holder
guide_agentcard_ch_li3: ✅ Chat with your agent directly from the card
guide_agentcard_ch_li4: ✅ View your agent's capabilities, protocols, and tags
guide_agentcard_tip_li1: ✅ Choose a catchy name: Pick a memorable name that reflects the agent's function
guide_agentcard_tip_li2: ✅ Be precise in description: Explain what the agent does in one sentence
guide_agentcard_tip_li3: ✅ List all capabilities: Help users judge if it meets their needs
guide_agentcard_tip_li4: ✅ Use accurate tags: Use common keywords for better discoverability
guide_agentcard_tip_li5: ✅ Update regularly: Sync the card when agent capabilities expand
guide_crossdevice_meta: The first gate for incoming messages: filtering, rate limiting, and access control in one place
guide_crossdevice_p_what: Cross-Device Messaging is the gateway that controls incoming external messages to your Bot via Public Code. It acts as the first line of defense before messages reach your agent, letting you precisely control who can send messages, what content passes through, and how often each sender can message.
guide_crossdevice_note_scenario: 💡 Configuration: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg. Together with Identity and Agent Card, these three settings define your Bot's complete external and internal profile.
guide_crossdevice_p_how: Incoming messages pass through 6 sequential checks: Card Holder block → Blacklist → Whitelist → Forbidden words → Media type → Rate limit. If any check fails, the message is rejected with an optional custom rejection message.
